questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) algorithm?
options: To minimize the loss function using a batch of data
To minimize the loss function using a single data point
To maximize the loss function using a batch of data
To minimize the loss function using a single data point
questions.explanation: SGD is an optimization algorithm that uses a single data point to compute the gradient of the loss function and update the model parameters. It is used for training models on small batches of data.

questions.question: Which optimization algorithm is known for its slow convergence rate?
options: Gradient Descent
Mini Batch Gradient Descent
Stochastic Gradient Descent
Newton-Raphson Method
questions.explanation: The Newton-Raphson method is an optimization algorithm that uses the Hessian matrix to compute the search direction. However, it can be computationally expensive and may not converge to the optimal solution.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Mini Batch Gradient Descent?
options: It is faster than Stochastic Gradient Descent
It is more accurate than Stochastic Gradient Descent
It reduces the variance of the gradient estimates
It is more computationally expensive than Stochastic Gradient Descent
questions.explanation: Mini Batch Gradient Descent reduces the variance of the gradient estimates by using a small batch of data to compute the gradient. This can lead to more accurate optimization results.

questions.question: Which optimization algorithm is an extension of the Gradient Descent algorithm?
options: Mini Batch Gradient Descent
Stochastic Gradient Descent
Newton-Raphson Method
Conjugate Gradient
questions.explanation: Mini Batch Gradient Descent is an extension of the Gradient Descent algorithm that uses a small batch of data to compute the gradient. It reduces the variance of the gradient estimates and can lead to more accurate optimization results.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Newton-Raphson Method?
options: To minimize the loss function using the gradient and Hessian
To maximize the loss function using the gradient and Hessian
To find the maximum likelihood estimate of a model
To find the minimum variance of a model
questions.explanation: The Newton-Raphson Method uses the gradient and Hessian of the loss function to compute the search direction. It is an efficient optimization algorithm, but it can be computationally expensive and may not converge to the optimal solution.
